codeReview.summary: The migration fix is complete for the local config hydration path. `getLocalConfig()` now normalizes unsupported `aiProvider` values to the default provider and resolves stale `aiModel` values through the current provider defaults, and the same normalization is applied when persisting local config and when hydrating daemon responses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared-state docs note the settings flow and AI provider pattern, but they do not document that `ConfigContext` must normalize unsupported `aiProvider` values and provider/model combinations with `normalizeConfig()` / `getModelForProvider()` when hydrating `vibogit-settings` from localStorage or daemon config.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/ui/src/lib/config-context.tsx:29-57 and 80-83 now normalize localStorage and daemon config; .factory/library/architecture.md only says settings flow uses localStorage and `getModelForProvider()` resolves models without recording this migration requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tauri-fullstack` skill assumes browser verification as a standard step even for small non-visual config-migration fixes, which did not match the actual work needed here and led the worker to explicitly deviate from the procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session b5c0db09-92d1-477f-815e-1fb27880601e records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` because step 6 manual browser verification was skipped for this non-visual config change; .factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md step 6 currently treats browser verification as part of the default workflow.

summary: Pass. The committed change fixes the previously missing Gemini/localStorage migration in `config-context.tsx`, keeps browser and daemon hydration aligned, and I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues in scope.
